items.question_text: Under 2-5-1, which is the correct way to describe a high altitude VOR/VORTAC/TACAN airway?
items.explanation: Per 2-5-1a, jet routes use the letter 'J' followed by the number in group form. Victor airways use 'Victor' followed by the number in group form. 'J Five Thirty-Three' is correct; 'Jet' is not used.

choices.text: 'Jet Five Thirty-Three.'
'J Five Thirty-Three.'
'J Five Three Three.'
'High Victor Five Thirty-Three.'

items.question_text: Under 2-5-1b, how is a VOR/VORTAC/TACAN alternate airway described?
items.explanation: Per 2-5-1b, alternate airways are described using the word 'Victor' followed by the airway number in group form and the alternate direction. The example given is 'Victor Twelve South.'

choices.text: Using 'J' followed by the number and direction.
'Victor' followed by the number in group form and the alternate direction.
'Alternate Victor' followed by the number.
The number followed by the word 'Alternate.'

items.question_text: Under 2-5-1c, colored/L/MF airways are described by stating the _____ followed by the number in group form.
items.explanation: Per 2-5-1c, colored/L/MF airways use the color of the airway followed by the number in group form, e.g., 'Blue Eighty-One.'

choices.text: color of the airway

items.question_text: Under 2-5-1e, how are ATS routes (letter-number routes) described?
items.explanation: Per 2-5-1e, ATS routes are described by stating the letter(s) of the route phonetically, followed by the number in group form. Examples include 'Romeo Twenty,' 'Alfa Fifty,' 'Golf Sixty-one,' and 'Alfa Seven Hundred.'

choices.text: State the letter(s) normally followed by the number digit-by-digit.
State the letter(s) phonetically, followed by the number in group form.
State the route name in plain language followed by the number.
State only the number; the letters are understood from context.

items.question_text: Under 2-5-1, how are published RNAV high altitude routes described?
items.explanation: Per 2-5-1g item 1, high altitude RNAV routes use the letter 'Q' followed by the route number in group form, e.g., 'Q One Forty-five.' Low altitude RNAV routes use the letter phonetically followed by the number in group form, e.g., 'Tango Two Ten.'

choices.text: State the letter 'Q' followed by the route number in group form.
State 'Quebec' phonetically followed by the route number.
State 'RNAV High' followed by the route number.
State the route number only; high altitude is implied.

items.question_text: Under 2-5-2a, when a NAVAID is used as a clearance limit, how must it be described?
items.explanation: Per 2-5-2a item 2, when used as a clearance limit, state the name of the NAVAID followed by the type of NAVAID if known. The phraseology is 'CLEARED TO (NAVAID name and type),' e.g., 'Cleared to Grand Rapids VOR.'

choices.text: Name of the NAVAID only; type is unnecessary.
Phonetic identifier only.
Name of the NAVAID followed by the type of NAVAID if known.
Type of NAVAID followed by the name.

items.question_text: Under 2-5-2b, when describing a radial from a VOR, you must state the separate digits of the radial followed by the word 'radial,' _____.
items.explanation: Per 2-5-2b item 1, the word 'degrees' is omitted when stating radials, azimuths, or bearings. The format is: NAVAID name followed by separate digits of the radial/azimuth/bearing and the word 'radial/azimuth/bearing.'

choices.text: omitting the word 'degrees'

items.question_text: Under 2-5-2b, which is the correct description of an arc about a VOR-DME NAVAID?
items.explanation: Per 2-5-2b item 2, arcs are described as: distance in miles, 'mile arc,' direction (eight principal compass points), 'of,' and the NAVAID name. Example: 'Two Zero mile arc southwest of Kirksville VOR.'

choices.text: 'Arc Two Zero miles from Kirksville VOR to the southwest.'
'Two Zero mile arc southwest of Kirksville VOR.'
'Kirksville VOR arc two zero southwest.'
'Southwest arc Twenty miles Kirksville VOR.'

items.question_text: Under 2-5-2b, how is a quadrant within a radius of a NAVAID described?
items.explanation: Per 2-5-2b item 3, quadrants are described as: direction in quadrant terms (NE, SE, SW, NW) followed by distance in miles from the NAVAID. Example: 'Cleared to fly northeast quadrant of Phillipsburg VORTAC within Four Zero mile radius.'

choices.text: Distance then quadrant then NAVAID name.
Quadrant direction, then distance in miles from the NAVAID.
NAVAID name then quadrant then distance.
Only the quadrant is needed; distance is implied.

items.question_text: Under 2-5-2b item 6, an NRS waypoint is described using the format: single letter corresponding to the ICAO FIR identifier, followed by the FIR subset letter, the latitude increment, and the longitude increment. Which is a correct example?
items.explanation: Per 2-5-2b item 6, an NRS waypoint uses the format: FIR letter + FIR subset letter + latitude increment + longitude increment. 'Kilo Delta Three Four Uniform' is correct. Latitude and longitude increments can be stated in single digit or group form.

choices.text: 'Kilo Delta Three Four Uniform.'
'NRS waypoint Kilo Delta.'
'Kilo Delta latitude Three Four longitude Uniform.'
'Waypoint Kilo Delta at Three Four by Uniform.'

items.question_text: Under 2-5-3a, when a fix is NOT named, how is it described?
items.explanation: Per 2-5-3a, unnamed fixes are described as: NAVAID name, specified radial/localizer/azimuth, distance in miles, and the phrase 'mile fix.' Example: 'Appleton Zero Five Zero radial Three Seven mile fix.'

choices.text: NAVAID name, radial, and the word 'intersection.'
NAVAID name, specified radial/localizer/azimuth, distance in miles, and 'mile fix.'
Distance and radial from the NAVAID without the NAVAID name.
Geographic coordinates if the fix is not charted.

items.question_text: Under 2-5-3b, how do you describe a fix that is charted on a SID, STAR, en route chart, or approach plate?
items.explanation: Per 2-5-3b, when a fix is charted, simply state the name of the fix. There is no need to describe it by radial and distance from a NAVAID.

choices.text: By radial and distance from the nearest VOR, regardless of charting.
State the name of the fix.
By latitude and longitude coordinates only.
By reference to the approach procedure name.

items.question_text: Under 2-5-3c, expressions such as 'passing Victor Twelve' or 'passing J Eleven' must _____.
items.explanation: Per 2-5-3c, controllers must use specific terms to describe a fix and must NOT use expressions such as 'passing Victor Twelve' or 'passing J Eleven.'

choices.text: not be used
